summary: Joel Spolsky provides a brief update on the volunteer translation effort for his blog articles. He reports that 22 languages now have volunteers and translations are starting to arrive. He outlines different levels of involvement available, from copy editing a single article to translating multi-part series. The post serves as a recruitment call for more bilingual volunteers to help make his content accessible internationally.
key_points: The translation initiative has attracted volunteers for 22 languages, with articles already being submitted
All translations go through a second copy editor review before publication, ensuring quality control
Joel structures volunteer opportunities by time commitment: copy editing (small), single article translation (medium), or multi-part series (large)
His most popular series like Painless Functional Specifications and UI For Programmers are priorities for translation
money_quotes: Copy editing basically means proofread it, correct any spelling or grammar mistakes, and rewrite anything that is not written clearly enough.

If you're really ambitious, you can sign up for the 4-part Painless Functional Specifications (still available in most languages) or the 9 chapter UI For Programmers, available in every language except French and Spanish.
key_insight: Building an international audience for technical writing can be scaled through structured volunteer translation programs with tiered commitment levels and editorial quality control.
